opportunities.rationale: FCX part of Basic Materials sector (XLB -5.84% WTD, MACD negative, below 50d MA) but is a copper producer with strong fundamental demand from AI/data-centre infrastructure build-out and EV transition. Price $41.47 (May 18 2026, XLB holdings). Non-structural shock — materials sector caught in defensive rotation, not copper-specific fundamental breakdown. RSI likely below 40. Invalidated below $36.50 if copper prices collapse below $4/lb or China demand weakens structurally.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on first green session after XLB stabilizes above $49; confirm FCX holds above $40.

opportunities.rationale: NUE (Nucor) down as part of materials sector rout (-5.84% WTD for XLB). Steel demand underpinned by US infrastructure spending; ISM Manufacturing 52.7 in April (4th straight expansionary month) signals industrial activity. Non-structural shock — sector rotation not fundamental. Price $137.75 (May 18, XLB holdings). RSI approaching oversold. Invalidated below $126 if PMI data surprises sharply to downside.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on flash PMI data May 21 if above 50; confirm NUE holds above 50d MA.

Invalidated below $265 if industrial gas demand shows structural weakness.

opportunities.rationale: XLB down -5.84% WTD, broke below 50d MA May 15 (MACD negative, momentum below 0). Sector rotation driven by defensive bid (energy/consumer defensive), not materials-specific structural breakdown. Bullish broad market regime (S&P above all MAs) creates mean-reversion opportunity at sector level. LOW conviction — MACD still negative; wait for first confirmed daily close above 50d MA before adding. Invalidated if Flash PMI (May 21) shows manufacturing contraction below 50.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Wait for first confirmed daily close above $50.50 after PMI data; do not enter before May 21.
